subject: Re: CPU RAM
body:
Depending on what you want to do, 16 GB might prove a bit limiting. 
TensorFlow's CPU memory consumption appears to be a multiple of the 
allocated GPU memory, and then there's the fixed footprint of running the 
OS, holding all particle metadata in memory when picking etc.

On Monday, August 13, 2018 at 10:34:23 AM UTC+2, Matthew Belousoff wrote:
>
> I've been running it fine on an old Skylake i5 with 16 Gb of RAM and a 
> single Titan (Maxwell). No dramas whatsover! So you can save your money if 
> you want.
>
> On Mon, 13 Aug 2018 at 17:11, Dimitry Tegunov <tegunov@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Mike,
>>
>> yes, 64 GB should definitely be fine, most likely even 48 GB. 
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Dimitry
>>
>>
>> On Monday, August 13, 2018 at 4:45:43 AM UTC+2, mcianfro@umich.edu wrote:
>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry, 
>>>
>>> Can you comment on the amount of CPU RAM required for a Warp machine? I 
>>> see that yours has 128GB RAM for 4 GPU cards, would it be safe to get 64 GB 
>>> RAM for 2 GPU cards? 
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Mike
>>>
